name: public_safety_workplace_injury_spike_oakline_auto_ben
premise: Wildfire evacuation routes are jammed; police want contraflow lanes that cut off hospital access from the east. In Public Safety, the situation tagged internally around public safety workplace injury spike oakline auto ben has converged with local pressure in the Oakline corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Engineering says a full fix needs a maintenance window; operations says waiting will predictably increase harm in the interim. Key facts are still arriving from the field; the team is working off stale dashboards and verbal handoffs. A hard operational cutoff hits in under twenty minutes unless a named executive posts a decision in the command log. The trade is not abstract: it is autonomy versus beneficence under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Hector Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Hector Ishikawa, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Public Safety. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat beneficence as disposable collateral every time autonomy spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Mateo Reed
agents.identity: I am Mateo Reed, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Public Safety. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so autonomy cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes beneficence in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if autonomy advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for beneficence.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Hector Ishikawa must choose under autonomy versus beneficence. What does Hector Ishikawa do?
